steps.title: Interpret the equation
steps.content: The equation is $10x + 31{,}480 = 32{,}210$. Its structure is starting population plus something times 10 years equals ending population.

steps.title: Connect x to the context
steps.content:
Since $x$ is multiplied by 10 years to give the total population change, $x$ is the change per year, or the average annual increase.

That matches Choice B — The average increase per year of the population between 2005 and 2015.

choiceElimination:
• Choice A — The total increase in population between 2005 and 2015: The total increase is 730 (= 32,210 − 31,480), which is 10x, not x itself. ✗
  • Choice B — The average increase per year of the population between 2005 and 2015: x = 73 is the total increase (730) divided by the number of years (10), giving the average annual increase. ✓
  • Choice C — The percentage by which the population of the town increased each year between 2005 and 2015: A percentage increase would require a multiplicative model (e.g., 31,480 × (1+x)^10), not an additive one like 10x. ✗
  • Choice D — The projected population of the town 10 years after 2015: The equation describes the change from 2005 to 2015, not a projection. x = 73 is clearly not a population figure. ✗
That matches Choice B — The average increase per year of the population between 2005 and 2015.
